source: Claude gap analysis 2026-06-11 (Track C) + PI.md Phase 9
title: Telegram becomes a peer client: same brain, own conversation, shared memory
current_state: Telegram proxies into the SAME in-memory message list as the desktop REPL — a phone message lands mid-desktop-conversation and vice versa. It is a notification channel wearing a chat costume.
target_state: Telegram chat maps to its own persistent conversation_id (telegram:<chat_id>) routed through the conversation store (T-186) — so desktop and phone are separate threads of short-term context sharing the same L2/L3 long-term memory. Switching devices mid-topic works via memory recall (as designed), not via accidental context splicing.
migration_plan: 1. TelegramTools handler: resolve conversation_id from chat_id, switch-restore-persist around process_input (same pattern as /chat in T-187; share the helper).
2. Bubble/thinking pipeline (T-123/T-124) unchanged — it wraps the text, not the conversation.
3. Tests: extend test_telegram_lifecycle — desktop turn then telegram turn → desktop conversation unchanged; telegram conversation persists across simulated restart.
risk_notes: Depends on T-186 conversation store. If T-187 lands first, route through its endpoint; otherwise call the store directly — either is fine, do not build a third path.
